title: pendingPermissionResult hangs if MainActivity destroyed during permission dialog
description: Trigger: user opens the app, triggers relay connect (BackgroundService.start() is called), the POST_NOTIFICATIONS system dialog appears, and the user rotates the screen or the system performs a configuration change. Android destroys and recreates MainActivity. pendingPermissionResult (MainActivity.kt:20) is an instance field — it is never called on the old Activity, so the Dart caller of invokeMethod('requestNotificationPermission') is blocked indefinitely. BackgroundService.start() is unawaited from RelayService._onData, so the relay itself continues, but the foreground service never starts for the rest of the session. The only recovery is manual disconnect+reconnect. Fix: result.error("ACTIVITY_DESTROYED", "Activity destroyed before permission result") in onDestroy, or store result in companion object keyed by request code.
